controlnote.p: Boxes: WR M a, WR M b, WR M c, WR M d, WR M e-WR 1 a, WR 1 b, WR 1 c, WR 1 d, WR 1 e, WR 1 f, WR 1 g, WR 1 h, WR 1 i, WR 1 j, WR 1 k, WR 1 l, WR 1 m, WR 1 n, WR 1 o, WR 1 p, WR 1 q, WR 1 r, WR 1 s, WR 1 t, WR 1 u, WR 1 v, WR 1 w, WR 1 x-WR 2 a, WR 2 b, WR 2 c, WR 2 d, WR 2 e-WR 3 a, WR 3 b-WR 4 a, WR 4 b-WR 5 a, WR 5 b, WR 5 c, WR 5 d, WR 5 e-WR 6 a, WR 6 b, WR 6 c, WR 6 d-WR 7 a, WR 7 b, WR 7 c, WR 7 d, WR 7 e-WR 8 a, WR 8 b, WR 2/7
This collection contains 16 published digital objects.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 25, 2025 at 20:00

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. The finding aid includes links to stream most interviews online via the AV access system, Aviary. Yale users can login to Aviary using their Yale University ID, to stream these interviews without restriction. Other users are able to request access to interviews directly through Aviary. For access instructions, and to request transcripts of an interview, see OHAM's website.
Some interviews may be restricted. Access to these restricted interviews may only be granted to a patron who has first obtained direct permission from the interviewee or their estate.
Some interviews may not have been digitized or are otherwise not available to stream online. However, these interviews may have been transcribed. Please see contact OHAM for more details.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: WR, Willie Ruff Interviews, Oral History of American Music, in the Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by surname of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Rights and permissions for OHAM materials vary between individual interviews. Researchers wishing to publish or broadcast OHAM materials are responsible for obtaining direct authorization from copyright holders: generally, the interviewees or their estates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interviews conducted by Willie Ruff with Bessie Jones at Yale university in New Haven, CT, as part of his research for the film The Beginnings of Bebop, with Dizzy Gillespie and Dwike Mitchell. In these interviews, Jones demonstrates African slave games.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interviews conducted by Willie Ruff with Bessie Jones at Yale university in New Haven, CT, as part of his research for the film The Beginnings of Bebop, with Dizzy Gillespie and Dwike Mitchell. In these interviews, Jones demonstrates African slave games.

scopecontent.p: Interviews conducted by Willie Ruff with Bessie Jones at Yale university in New Haven, CT, as part of his research for the film The Beginnings of Bebop, with Dizzy Gillespie and Dwike Mitchell. In these interviews, Jones demonstrates African slave games.
